text: When working with APIs, you send HTTP requests to a server to get or send data. Most modern APIs return data in JSON format, which is easy for programs to read. To access many APIs, you need authentication, often using an API key. This key is included in your request so the server knows who you are. Be aware that APIs often have rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time. Exceeding these limits may result in an error. Therefore, it is important to implement error handling in your code. This helps you manage problems like failed requests or incorrect responses and keep your application running smoothly.
